title: agrostis flaccida subsp. trinii
intro: Agrostis flaccida subsp. trinii, also known as Trin's bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Trin's bentgrass has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, with its roots and leaves being used to prepare herbal remedies. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and aiding in the regulation of bowel movements. In herbal preparations, Trin's bentgrass is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
